vehicle.label: Long-Range Surveillance Wing
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing flying-wing UAV, 2m wingspan, 4+ hour endurance

parts.label: Center Body Pod
parts.spec: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

parts.label: Wing Rib Set x 12
parts.spec: Wing rib NACA airfoil profile, lightening pockets, leading edge sock, trailing edge taper

parts.label: Elevon Servo Pocket x 2
parts.spec: Elevon servo pocket, MG90S or DS215MG, control horn linkage, removable access cover

parts.label: Motor + Pusher Prop Mount
parts.spec: Rear pusher 2814 BLDC mount, 10x7 folding prop clearance, thrust line +1deg down

parts.label: GPS + Pitot Boom
parts.spec: Nose boom 8mm, GPS at tip, pitot static port forward, BEC airflow shielded

parts.label: Camera Gimbal Bay
parts.spec: Forward downward camera gimbal bay 70mm deep, 2-axis micro gimbal, optical window

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide)
parts.spec: 4S 16000mAh Li-ion slide-in bay, CG adjust slot, top access hatch, balance lead window

parts.label: Integration Service Backbone
parts.spec: Wing/fuselage integration service backbone with assembly_datum pads, harness_raceway, service_hatch, connector_keying, anti-rotation wing datum, and visible load-path inspection marks

generated_parts.label: Center Body Pod

verify.rule: Ensure the blueprint adheres to the specified schema.
Ensure the mass estimate is within the budget.
Ensure all P0 internal_feature_targets appear in part_tree/geometry_ops.
Ensure interfaces with neighboring subsystems are reciprocal.
Ensure critical joints have visible locking methods and preload evidence.
Ensure boundary conditions are specified for downstream CFD/FEA.

risk.desc: The cooling inlet may not provide sufficient airflow under high ambient temperatures.
risk.mit: Increase the size of the cooling inlet or add additional vents.

risk.desc: The service access panel may be difficult to open in tight spaces.
risk.mit: Design the service access panel to be removable without tools.

risk.desc: The structural reinforcement may not be sufficient for the wing bending loads.
risk.mit: Increase the thickness of the reinforcement or add additional struts.

risk.desc: The harness path may be too tight, causing damage to the wiring.
risk.mit: Increase the size of the harness path or use flexible wiring.

risk.desc: The motor mount may not be able to withstand the torque of the motor.
risk.mit: Increase the size of the motor mount or use stronger fasteners.

cooling air from rear motor pod to pod interior
heat from equipment bay to pod skin
internal pressure from equipment bay to pod skin
power and data from pod to motor pod

risk.desc: Potential thermal expansion issues due to heat-resistant belly and FLIR bay.
risk.mit: Use thermally stable materials and conductive paths to manage heat.

risk.desc: Risk of improper fastener torque leading to joint failure.
risk.mit: Implement torque wrenches and standardized torque values.

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment during assembly due to complex interfaces.
risk.mit: Use alignment pins and guides during assembly.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient clearance for moving parts.
risk.mit: Ensure all critical interfaces have appropriate clearances.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure due to high load conditions.
risk.mit: Conduct stress analysis and reinforce critical load paths.

risk.mit: Use heat-resistant materials and conductive paths for thermal management

risk.desc: Risk of improper load path continuity if spar socket is not properly aligned
risk.mit: Ensure spar socket is clearly marked and accessible for alignment

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient cooling airflow if channel dimensions are not optimized
risk.mit: Optimize channel dimensions for maximum airflow and cooling efficiency

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure if fastener preload is not properly applied
risk.mit: Specify torque_Nm for fastener installation and ensure preload is checked

risk.mit: Define primary datum feature and ensure it is accessible for alignment

requirements: Elevon servo pocket for MG90S or DS215MG servo
Fixed-wing flying-wing UAV, 2m wingspan, 4+ hour endurance
Wildfire monitoring, heat-resistant belly, real-time video relay, FLIR bay

verify.value: All required fields are present.
verify.rule: Ensure all required fields are included in the blueprint.
Ensure the mass estimate is within the subsystem's mass budget.

verify.value: All P0 features are mapped to geometry_ops.
verify.rule: Ensure all P0 internal_feature_targets appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets.

verify.value: All interfaces have reciprocal definitions in adjacent subsystems.
verify.rule: Ensure adjacent interfaces are named and defined with connection_type, DOF, clearance_mm, and required_feature.

verify.value: Visible locking evidence exists for critical joints.
verify.rule: Ensure critical joints have visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence.
Ensure boundary_conditions include fixture_points, thermal_zones, and pressure_faces.

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress due to high servo heat dissipation.
risk.mit: Implement thermal management solutions such as heat sinks or cooling channels.

risk.desc: Risk of cable strain during assembly or operation.
risk.mit: Ensure all cable exits have strain relief geometry and adhere to minimum bend radius requirements.

risk.desc: Risk of mis-mating connectors due to lack of keying or labeling.
risk.mit: Add keying or labeling to connectors to prevent mis-mating.

risk.desc: Risk of improper alignment due to lack of primary datum feature.
risk.mit: Define a primary datum feature such as a flat, bore, or pin.

risk.desc: Risk of assembly errors due to ambiguous assembly sequence.
risk.mit: Define a clear assembly_sequence with tool, torque, and datum per step.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: payload - role: Non-weapon camera/gimbal sensor pod bay with removable insert and window

physics_paths.description: Structural loads from wing bending and servo actuation are transferred to the sensor bay.
Heat from the camera and gimbal electronics is dissipated through the thermal dissipation path to the wing structure.
Power and data signals are routed from the power distribution bus to the camera pod.
Human access for maintenance and servicing is provided through the service access window.
Motion from the gimbal motor is transferred to the gimbal mount to control camera orientation.

integration_opportunities.description: Connect the camera pod to the electronics bay for power and data.
Provide access for harnesses and cables through the service access window.
Mount the camera gimbal bay to the wing structure using the spar socket.

manufacturing_strategy.support: orientation: vertical, inspection: visual and tactile
manufacturing_strategy.implications: Ensure spar_socket remains visible and inspectable during manufacturing.
